metadata.description: Cluster name. Used as resource-group suffix and tag.
Azure region.
Existing resource group name to use. If empty, a new RG named rg-azcluster-<clusterName> is created.
SSH public key for admin user on all VMs.
Admin username on VMs.
Scheduler VM SKU.
Login VM SKU.
Ubuntu HPC marketplace SKU.
Give login VM a public IP. Default false. Opt-in for testing.
Optional NSG narrowing for SSH when login VM has a public IP. Empty means SSH allowed from Internet.
azcluster release tag to fetch binaries/assets from GitHub Releases.
GitHub org/repo to fetch releases from. Phase 0 default is the upstream repo.
VNet address space.
ANF capacity pool size in TiB. Minimum 1 (Premium/Ultra) or 2 (Standard).
ANF service level.
AMLFS (Azure Managed Lustre) capacity in TiB. 0 disables AMLFS.
AMLFS SKU. Throughput is MB/s per TiB.
Availability zone for AMLFS.
Compute pools. Each: { name: string, sku: string, count: int, default: bool }. Default: one gpu pool, H100, 0.
Provision Azure Managed Prometheus (AMW + DCR + AMA) and Azure Managed Grafana for the cluster. Off by default.
Azure region for Managed Grafana. Defaults to the cluster location. Override when the cluster region does not host Managed Grafana (e.g. southafricanorth -> uksouth).
AAD object id of the deployer (user or service principal). Granted Grafana Admin on AMG (when monitoring is on) and Key Vault Secrets Officer on the per-cluster vault. Required.
Principal type of deployerPrincipalId.
Shared filesystem backing /shared. `anf` provisions Azure NetApp Files; `nfs-scheduler` exports /shared from the scheduler VM (test-only, no HA).
Provision Slurm accounting (Azure Database for MySQL Flexible Server + slurmdbd on the scheduler).
MySQL Flexible Server admin password for the Slurm accounting database. Generated by the CLI when enableAccounting is true; ignored otherwise.
LDAP admin password for cn=admin,dc=azcluster,dc=local. Generated by the CLI on every deploy.
Space-joined list of extra apt packages to install on every node (scheduler, login, compute). Each token validated CLI-side against ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.+-]*$.
Provision an Azure Bastion (Standard SKU with native client tunneling) for SSH access without a public IP on login.
Globally unique Key Vault name (3-24 chars). Generated by the CLI from blake3(subscription_id || cluster_name || location).
Provision a per-cluster storage account with a single container `data`. Default on. When off, no storage resources, env vars, or examples are provisioned.

Globally unique storage account name (3-24 lowercase alphanumeric). Generated by the CLI from blake3(subscription_id || cluster_name || location) when --storage-name is not given. Ignored when enableStorage is false. Empty string when storage is disabled. Length is validated inside modules/storage.bicep when storage is enabled.
Enable Hierarchical Namespace (ADLS Gen2) on the storage account. Default off. When true, a dfs Private Endpoint sub-resource is also provisioned.
Allow public network access on the storage account. Default false (Private Endpoint only). Set true to expose the account to the operator laptop and skip Private Endpoint/DNS provisioning.
Storage account SKU.
Storage account default access tier. Ignored for Premium SKUs.
azcp version pinned in cloud-init for login + compute. Installed from https://github.com/edwardsp/azcp/releases/<version>/azcp-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz.
Deploy timestamp in UTC ISO8601, stamped into the azcluster:deployed-at RG tag for operator-facing discovery.
Globally unique Key Vault name. Generated by the CLI from blake3(subscription_id || cluster_name || location).
Azure region.
AAD object id of the deployer (user or service principal). Granted Key Vault Secrets Officer on this vault.
Principal type of deployerPrincipalId.
Tenant id the vault belongs to. Defaults to the deployment subscription tenant.
Tags applied to the vault.
Globally unique storage account name. Generated by the CLI from blake3(subscription_id || cluster_name || location), or overridden via --storage-name. 3-24 lowercase alphanumeric.
Azure region.
Enable Hierarchical Namespace (ADLS Gen2). When true, the dfs private endpoint sub-resource is also provisioned.
Storage account SKU.
Default access tier for the Blob service. Ignored for Premium accounts.
Allow public network access to the storage account. When false, only the Private Endpoint is reachable.
Resource id of the user-assigned managed identity that will be granted Storage Blob Data Contributor on the account. Compute + login VMs authenticate via this UAI through IMDS.
Resource id of the compute subnet where the Private Endpoint NIC will live.
Resource id of the VNet that the Private DNS zones will be linked to.
Tags applied to all resources in this module.
Cluster name; used as suffix for the MySQL Flexible Server.
Azure region for the MySQL Flexible Server. Must match the cluster region.
Resource ID of the delegated subnet for MySQL Flexible Server VNet integration.
MySQL Flexible Server SKU. Standard_B2ms is the cheapest burstable option that supports slurmdbd.
SKU tier.
Storage size in GiB for the MySQL Flexible Server data disk.
MySQL admin login name. slurmdbd does not use this; it is the server admin.
MySQL admin password. Generated by the CLI and passed as a secure parameter.
Slurm accounting database name.
Resource tags.

variables.cloudInitTemplate:
#cloud-config
hostname: {{CLUSTER_NAME}}-scheduler
manage_etc_hosts: true
fqdn: {{CLUSTER_NAME}}-scheduler

bootcmd:
  - systemctl stop unattended-upgrades.service apt-daily.service apt-daily-upgrade.service apt-daily.timer apt-daily-upgrade.timer 2>/dev/null || true
  - systemctl mask unattended-upgrades.service apt-daily.service apt-daily-upgrade.service apt-daily.timer apt-daily-upgrade.timer 2>/dev/null || true
  - pkill -9 -f 'unattended-upgrade|apt-get|apt\.systemd\.daily' 2>/dev/null || true

package_update: true
package_upgrade: false

packages:
  - curl
  - ca-certificates
  - jq
  - nfs-common
  - munge
  - libmunge2
  - debconf-utils
  - ldap-utils

write_files:
  - path: /etc/azcluster/install.env
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      AZCLUSTER_VERSION={{AZCLUSTER_VERSION}}
      AZCLUSTER_REPO={{AZCLUSTER_REPO}}
      AZCLUSTER_NAME={{CLUSTER_NAME}}
      AZCLUSTER_ROLE=scheduler
      SHARED_MOUNT_IP={{SHARED_MOUNT_IP}}
      SHARED_EXPORT_PATH={{SHARED_EXPORT_PATH}}
      RUN_NFS_SERVER={{RUN_NFS_SERVER}}
      ENABLE_ACCOUNTING={{ENABLE_ACCOUNTING}}
      ACCT_MYSQL_FQDN={{ACCT_MYSQL_FQDN}}
      ACCT_MYSQL_USER={{ACCT_MYSQL_USER}}
      ACCT_MYSQL_DB={{ACCT_MYSQL_DB}}
      LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=azcluster,dc=local
      EXTRA_PACKAGES="{{EXTRA_PACKAGES}}"
      ADMIN_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY="{{ADMIN_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}}"
  - path: /etc/azcluster/accounting.password
    permissions: '0600'
    owner: 'root:root'
    content: |
      {{ACCT_MYSQL_PASSWORD}}
  - path: /etc/azcluster/ldap.password
    permissions: '0600'
    owner: 'root:root'
    content: |
      {{LDAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD}}
  - path: /etc/azcluster/openssh-lpk.ldif
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      dn: cn=openssh-lpk,cn=schema,cn=config
      objectClass: olcSchemaConfig
      cn: openssh-lpk
      olcAttributeTypes: ( 1.3.6.1.4.1.24552.500.1.1.1.13
        NAME 'sshPublicKey' DESC 'MANDATORY: OpenSSH Public key'
        EQUALITY octetStringMatch
        SYNTAX 1.3.6.1.4.1.1466.115.121.1.40 )
      olcObjectClasses: ( 1.3.6.1.4.1.24552.500.1.1.2.0
        NAME 'ldapPublicKey' SUP top AUXILIARY
        DESC 'MANDATORY: OpenSSH LPK objectclass'
        MAY ( sshPublicKey $ uid ) )
  - path: /etc/azcluster/ldap-base.ldif.tmpl
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      dn: ou=people,__BASE_DN__
      objectClass: organizationalUnit
      ou: people

      dn: ou=groups,__BASE_DN__
      objectClass: organizationalUnit
      ou: groups

      dn: cn=azusers,ou=groups,__BASE_DN__
      objectClass: posixGroup
      cn: azusers
      gidNumber: 20000

      dn: cn=cluster-admins,ou=groups,__BASE_DN__
      objectClass: posixGroup
      cn: cluster-admins
      gidNumber: 20100

      dn: cn=uidNext,__BASE_DN__
      objectClass: device
      objectClass: extensibleObject
      cn: uidNext
      uidNumber: 20003

      dn: uid=clusteradmin,ou=people,__BASE_DN__
      objectClass: inetOrgPerson
      objectClass: posixAccount
      objectClass: shadowAccount
      objectClass: ldapPublicKey
      cn: clusteradmin
      sn: clusteradmin
      uid: clusteradmin
      uidNumber: 20001
      gidNumber: 20100
      homeDirectory: /shared/home/clusteradmin
      loginShell: /bin/bash
      sshPublicKey: __ADMIN_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY__

      dn: cn=cluster-admins,ou=groups,__BASE_DN__
      changetype: modify
      add: memberUid
      memberUid: clusteradmin

      dn: uid=clusteruser,ou=people,__BASE_DN__
      objectClass: inetOrgPerson
      objectClass: posixAccount
      objectClass: shadowAccount
      objectClass: ldapPublicKey
      cn: clusteruser
      sn: clusteruser
      uid: clusteruser
      uidNumber: 20002
      gidNumber: 20000
      homeDirectory: /shared/home/clusteruser
      loginShell: /bin/bash
      sshPublicKey: __ADMIN_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY__
  - path: /etc/systemd/system/azcluster-server.service
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      [Unit]
      Description=azcluster daemon
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target

      [Service]
      Type=simple
      ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/azcluster-server
      Restart=on-failure
      RestartSec=5
      Environment=AZCLUSTER_BIND=0.0.0.0:8443
      Environment=AZCLUSTER_CLUSTER_NAME={{CLUSTER_NAME}}
      Environment=RUST_LOG=info

      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target
  - path: /etc/apt/preferences.d/slurm-repository-pin-990
    permissions: '0644'
    content: |
      Package: slurm-smd*
      Pin: origin "packages.microsoft.com"
      Pin-Priority: 990
  - path: /opt/azcluster/install-scheduler.sh
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      set -euo pipefail
      # Defense-in-depth: cloud-init runcmd inherits a 0077 umask so heredocs
      # produce 0600 files. The chmod block near the end of this script fixes
      # that, but a mid-script abort (e.g. transient curl 404 fetching the
      # release tarball) would leave /etc/slurm/*.conf at 0600 - breaks every
      # non-root srun/sinfo. Trap on EXIT to guarantee chmod runs regardless.
      trap 'chmod 0644 /etc/slurm/slurm.conf /etc/slurm/plugstack.conf /etc/slurm/cgroup.conf /etc/slurm/gres.conf 2>/dev/null || true' EXIT
      . /etc/azcluster/install.env

      if [ -n "${EXTRA_PACKAGES:-}" ]; then
        apt-get -o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=600 install -y ${EXTRA_PACKAGES}
      fi

      mkdir -p /shared /opt/azcluster /var/log/azcluster /var/spool/slurmctld /var/log/slurm /etc/slurm /opt/pyxis

      if [ "${RUN_NFS_SERVER:-false}" = "false" ]; then
        if ! grep -q " /shared " /etc/fstab; then
          echo "${SHARED_MOUNT_IP}:/${SHARED_EXPORT_PATH} /shared nfs rw,hard,rsize=262144,wsize=262144,vers=4.1,tcp,_netdev 0 0" >> /etc/fstab
        fi
        mountpoint -q /shared || mount /shared
      fi

      SCHED_DIR="/shared/sched/${AZCLUSTER_NAME}"
      mkdir -p "${SCHED_DIR}"

      curl -fsSL https://packages.microsoft.com/keys/microsoft.asc | gpg --batch --yes --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/microsoft-prod.gpg
      ARCH=$(dpkg --print-architecture)
      CODENAME=$(. /etc/os-release; echo "${VERSION_CODENAME}")
      echo "deb [arch=${ARCH} signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/microsoft-prod.gpg] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/slurm-ubuntu-${CODENAME} stable main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/slurm.list
      EXTRA_PKGS=""
      if [ "${RUN_NFS_SERVER:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        EXTRA_PKGS="${EXTRA_PKGS} nfs-kernel-server"
      fi
      if [ "${ENABLE_ACCOUNTING:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        EXTRA_PKGS="${EXTRA_PKGS} slurm-smd-slurmdbd mariadb-client"
      fi
      apt-get -o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=600 update
      apt-get -o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=600 install -y --no-install-recommends \
        slurm-smd \
        slurm-smd-client \
        slurm-smd-slurmctld \
        slurm-smd-libnss-slurm \
        ${EXTRA_PKGS}

      if [ "${RUN_NFS_SERVER:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        chmod 1777 /shared
        if ! grep -q "^/shared " /etc/exports; then
          echo "/shared *(rw,sync,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash)" >> /etc/exports
        fi
        systemctl enable --now nfs-server
        exportfs -rav
      fi

      getent group slurm >/dev/null || groupadd -g 11100 slurm
      id slurm >/dev/null 2>&1 || useradd -u 11100 -g 11100 -d /var/lib/slurm -s /usr/sbin/nologin -c "Slurm" slurm
      chown -R slurm:slurm /var/spool/slurmctld /var/log/slurm

      if [ ! -f "${SCHED_DIR}/munge.key" ]; then
        umask 077
        dd if=/dev/urandom of="${SCHED_DIR}/munge.key" bs=1 count=1024
        chown munge:munge "${SCHED_DIR}/munge.key"
        chmod 0400 "${SCHED_DIR}/munge.key"
      fi
      install -o munge -g munge -m 0400 "${SCHED_DIR}/munge.key" /etc/munge/munge.key

      cat > /etc/slurm/slurm.conf <<EOF
      ClusterName=${AZCLUSTER_NAME}
      SlurmctldHost=${AZCLUSTER_NAME}-scheduler
      SlurmUser=slurm
      AuthType=auth/munge
      CryptoType=crypto/munge
      StateSaveLocation=/var/spool/slurmctld
      SlurmdSpoolDir=/var/spool/slurmd
      SlurmctldPort=6817
      SlurmdPort=6818
      SlurmctldLogFile=/var/log/slurm/slurmctld.log
      SlurmdLogFile=/var/log/slurm/slurmd.log
      ReturnToService=2
      ProctrackType=proctrack/cgroup
      TaskPlugin=task/affinity,task/cgroup
      SelectType=select/cons_tres
      SelectTypeParameters=CR_Core_Memory
      GresTypes=gpu
      SchedulerType=sched/backfill
      MpiDefault=none
      LaunchParameters=enable_nss_slurm
      SlurmctldParameters=enable_configless
      MaxNodeCount=128
      TreeWidth=65533
      PlugStackConfig=/etc/slurm/plugstack.conf
      HealthCheckProgram=/usr/local/sbin/azcluster-healthcheck
      HealthCheckInterval=300
      HealthCheckNodeState=ANY,CYCLE
      Prolog=/etc/slurm/prolog.d/10-azcluster-user-nvme.sh
      PrologFlags=Alloc,Contain
      JobAcctGatherType=jobacct_gather/cgroup
      JobAcctGatherFrequency=30
      {{PARTITIONS}}
      EOF

      if [ "${ENABLE_ACCOUNTING:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        cat >> /etc/slurm/slurm.conf <<EOF
      AccountingStorageType=accounting_storage/slurmdbd
      AccountingStorageHost=${AZCLUSTER_NAME}-scheduler
      AccountingStoragePort=6819
      AccountingStorageEnforce=associations,limits,qos
      AccountingStorageTRES=gres/gpu
      EOF
      fi

      cat > /etc/slurm/cgroup.conf <<EOF
      CgroupPlugin=cgroup/v2
      ConstrainCores=yes
      ConstrainRAMSpace=yes
      ConstrainDevices=yes
      EOF

      # Configless Slurm distributes slurm.conf to clients but NOT gres.conf;
      # however slurmctld still parses its local copy to validate GresTypes.
      # AutoDetect=nvml works because slurm-smd is built with NVML support.
      cat > /etc/slurm/gres.conf <<EOF
      AutoDetect=nvml
      EOF
      chown slurm:slurm /etc/slurm/gres.conf

      mkdir -p /opt/pyxis
      BASE="https://github.com/${AZCLUSTER_REPO}/releases/download/${AZCLUSTER_VERSION}"
      curl -fsSL --retry 8 --retry-delay 5 -o /opt/pyxis/spank_pyxis.so "${BASE}/spank_pyxis-${AZCLUSTER_VERSION}-x86_64-linux.so"
      chmod 0755 /opt/pyxis/spank_pyxis.so

      cat > /etc/slurm/plugstack.conf <<EOF
      required /opt/pyxis/spank_pyxis.so runtime_path=/var/lib/enroot/runtime
      EOF

      chown -R slurm:slurm /etc/slurm
      # cloud-init runcmd inherits a 0077 umask, so `cat >` produces 0600 files.
      # The configs MUST be 0644 - srun/sinfo run as the submitting user and
      # parse /etc/slurm/slurm.conf + plugstack.conf locally (Pyxis spank loads
      # at submit time, so plugstack.conf must be world-readable too).
      chmod 0644 /etc/slurm/slurm.conf /etc/slurm/plugstack.conf /etc/slurm/cgroup.conf /etc/slurm/gres.conf

      EXAMPLES_DIR=/shared/examples
      mkdir -p "${EXAMPLES_DIR}"
      cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/hostname.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      #SBATCH --job-name=hostname
      #SBATCH --output=hostname-%j.out
      #SBATCH --nodes=1
      #SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=1
      srun hostname
      EOF
      cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/pyxis-alpine.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      #SBATCH --job-name=pyxis-alpine
      #SBATCH --output=pyxis-alpine-%j.out
      #SBATCH --nodes=1
      #SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=1
      srun --container-image=docker://alpine:latest hostname
      EOF
      cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/gpu-smi.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      #SBATCH --job-name=gpu-smi
      #SBATCH --output=gpu-smi-%j.out
      #SBATCH --nodes=1
      #SBATCH --gres=gpu:1
      srun nvidia-smi
      EOF
      cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/nccl-allreduce.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      #SBATCH --job-name=nccl-allreduce
      #SBATCH --output=nccl-allreduce-%j.out
      #SBATCH --nodes=2
      #SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=8
      #SBATCH --gpus-per-node=8
      #SBATCH --exclusive

      # Multi-node NCCL all-reduce on Azure NDv5 H100, bare-metal,
      # using HPC-X (PMIx 4.x) + NCCL preinstalled in the
      # microsoft-dsvm:ubuntu-hpc:2404 image. Expect ~340 GB/s avg /
      # ~460 GB/s peak busbw at 16 GiB across 8x NDR400 IB.
      #
      # For the same test inside an NGC container (Pyxis), see
      # dgxc-nemo-multinode-smoke.sbatch (added in v0.13.6).

      HPCX_DIR=$(ls -d /opt/hpcx-*-gcc-doca_ofed-ubuntu24.04-cuda*-x86_64 2>/dev/null | head -1)
      if [ -z "${HPCX_DIR}" ]; then
        echo "HPC-X not found under /opt — wrong image?" >&2
        exit 1
      fi
      source "${HPCX_DIR}/hpcx-init.sh"
      hpcx_load

      export NCCL_DEBUG=INFO
      export NCCL_IB_HCA=mlx5_ib
      export NCCL_TOPO_FILE=/opt/microsoft/ndv5-topo.xml
      export UCX_NET_DEVICES=mlx5_ib0:1,mlx5_ib1:1,mlx5_ib2:1,mlx5_ib3:1,mlx5_ib4:1,mlx5_ib5:1,mlx5_ib6:1,mlx5_ib7:1

      srun --mpi=pmix /opt/nccl-tests/build/all_reduce_perf \
        -b 8M -e 16G -f 2 -g 1
      EOF
      cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/dgxc-nemo-container-smoke.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      #SBATCH --job-name=dgxc-nemo-smoke
      #SBATCH --output=dgxc-nemo-smoke-%j.out
      #SBATCH --partition=gpu
      #SBATCH --nodes=1
      #SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=1
      #SBATCH --gpus-per-node=8
      #SBATCH --exclusive
      #SBATCH --time=00:30:00

      # DGXC infra smoke test on Azure NDv5 H100.
      # Single node, 8 GPUs, in-container NCCL all-reduce inside the
      # production DGXC NeMo container. ~10 minutes wall-clock (most of
      # which is the first-time 20 GB container import onto /mnt/nvme).
      #
      # WHAT IT TESTS (the v0.13.5 surface):
      #   * NVMe RAID-0 ephemeral scratch (/mnt/nvme) backing Enroot import
      #     of the ~20 GB NeMo container -- seconds vs minutes on /var/lib
      #   * Pyxis container launch with 8 H100 visible inside
      #   * /etc/enroot/environ.d/50-nccl.env propagating NCCL_IB_HCA /
      #     NCCL_TOPO_FILE / UCX_NET_DEVICES into the container
      #   * /etc/enroot/mounts.d/50-azcluster.fstab bind-mounting
      #     /opt/microsoft (NDv5 topology XML) into the container
      #   * NCCL all-reduce across 8 H100 using NVLink + InfiniBand HCA
      #     from inside the NGC NeMo container (IBext_v11 / SHARP)
      #
      # WHY SINGLE NODE: this is the infra smoke. For the multi-node
      # containerised NCCL all-reduce (v0.13.6+) see
      # dgxc-nemo-multinode-smoke.sbatch.
      #
      # WHY NOT "RUN LLAMA 8B": NeMo / Megatron-Bridge recipe APIs
      # rename between container releases (llama31_8b_pretrain_config,
      # llama3_8b factory, pretrain_recipe, etc.). The full DGXC Llama
      # training flow is documented in walkthrough-dgxc.md via NVIDIA's
      # llmb-run driver, which pins exact versions and handles the API
      # surface for you. This sbatch is the deterministic infra check.
      #
      # RUN:
      #   sbatch /shared/examples/dgxc-nemo-container-smoke.sbatch
      #   tail -f dgxc-nemo-smoke-<jobid>.out
      # Success criteria:
      #   * "pyxis: imported docker image: ..." line appears
      #   * "rank 0 / world 8" prints from inside the container
      #   * "all_reduce avg busbw" prints with a value > 100 GB/s
      #   * NCCL_DEBUG INFO mentions IBext_v11 + mlx5_ib (NOT 'via SOCKET')

      NEMO_IMAGE=${NEMO_IMAGE:-nvcr.io/nvidia/nemo:25.07.02}

      mkdir -p /shared/dgxc
      cat > /shared/dgxc/nccl_allreduce_smoke.py <<'PY'
      import os, time, torch
      import torch.distributed as dist

      def main():
          local_rank = int(os.environ["LOCAL_RANK"])
          torch.cuda.set_device(local_rank)
          dist.init_process_group(backend="nccl")
          rank, world = dist.get_rank(), dist.get_world_size()
          print(f"rank {rank} / world {world} on device {torch.cuda.current_device()} "
                f"({torch.cuda.get_device_name(local_rank)})", flush=True)

          # Warmup + measured all-reduce of a 1 GiB float16 tensor.
          numel = 512 * 1024 * 1024          # 512 M elements = 1 GiB fp16
          tensor = torch.ones(numel, dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
          for _ in range(5):
              dist.all_reduce(tensor)
          torch.cuda.synchronize()

          iters = 20
          dist.barrier()
          t0 = time.perf_counter()
          for _ in range(iters):
              dist.all_reduce(tensor)
          torch.cuda.synchronize()
          elapsed = time.perf_counter() - t0

          if rank == 0:
              size_bytes = numel * 2
              # busbw factor for ring/tree all-reduce = 2*(N-1)/N
              algbw = size_bytes * iters / elapsed / 1e9
              busbw = algbw * 2 * (world - 1) / world
              print(f"all_reduce size=1GiB iters={iters} elapsed={elapsed:.3f}s "
                    f"algbw={algbw:.2f} GB/s avg busbw={busbw:.2f} GB/s",
                    flush=True)
          dist.destroy_process_group()

      if __name__ == "__main__":
          main()
      PY

      srun \
        --container-image="${NEMO_IMAGE}" \
        --container-mounts=/shared:/shared,/mnt/nvme:/mnt/nvme \
        --no-container-mount-home \
        --export=ALL,NCCL_DEBUG=INFO \
        bash -c 'set -euo pipefail; cd /; torchrun --nproc_per_node=8 /shared/dgxc/nccl_allreduce_smoke.py'
      EOF
      cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/dgxc-nemo-multinode-smoke.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/usr/bin/env bash
      #SBATCH --job-name=dgxc-nemo-multi
      #SBATCH --output=dgxc-nemo-multi-%j.out
      #SBATCH --partition=gpu
      #SBATCH --nodes=2
      #SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=8
      #SBATCH --gpus-per-node=8
      #SBATCH --exclusive
      #SBATCH --time=00:30:00

      # Multi-node containerised NCCL all-reduce on Azure NDv5 H100.
      # 2 nodes x 8 H100 = 16 ranks across 8x NDR400 InfiniBand per node.
      #
      # This is the cross-node Pyxis test that v0.13.6 unblocked. Pre-
      # v0.13.6 this failed with isolated single-node MPI worlds because
      # slurmd had no PMIX_MCA_* env and Enroot wasn't propagating
      # PMIX_* into the container or bind-mounting PMIX_SERVER_TMPDIR.
      # v0.13.6 wires both: slurmd EnvironmentFile carries PMIX_MCA_*
      # and /etc/enroot/hooks.d/50-slurm-pmi.sh handles propagation.
      #
      # WHAT IT TESTS:
      #   * srun --mpi=pmix launches 16 ranks across 2 containers
      #   * 50-slurm-pmi.sh hook copies PMIX_* + SLURM_* into container
      #   * PMIX_SERVER_TMPDIR bind-mounted -> ranks see ONE PMIx world
      #   * NCCL discovers 8x mlx5_ib NICs per node, IBext / SHARP
      #   * All-reduce busbw across IB (expect > 100 GB/s avg @ 1 GiB)
      #
      # RUN:
      #   sbatch /shared/examples/dgxc-nemo-multinode-smoke.sbatch
      # Success criteria:
      #   * "rank 0 / world 16" and "rank 8 / world 16" both print
      #   * "all_reduce avg busbw" prints with a value > 100 GB/s
      #   * NCCL_DEBUG INFO shows "NET/IB" + "mlx5_ib" + "via SHARP"
      #     (NOT "via SOCKET" - that means PMIx fell back to TCP)

      NEMO_IMAGE=${NEMO_IMAGE:-nvcr.io/nvidia/nemo:25.07.02}

      # Reuse the same all-reduce script as the single-node smoke.
      if [ ! -f /shared/dgxc/nccl_allreduce_smoke.py ]; then
        echo "Missing /shared/dgxc/nccl_allreduce_smoke.py; run dgxc-nemo-container-smoke.sbatch first to drop it." >&2
        exit 1
      fi

      # srun --mpi=pmix is the key: it makes Slurm spawn one container
      # rank per task with PMIX_* env populated; the enroot hook copies
      # those into the container and bind-mounts the PMIx server socket.
      srun --mpi=pmix \
        --container-image="${NEMO_IMAGE}" \
        --container-mounts=/shared:/shared,/mnt/nvme:/mnt/nvme \
        --no-container-mount-home \
        --export=ALL,NCCL_DEBUG=INFO \
        bash -c 'set -euo pipefail; cd /; python /shared/dgxc/nccl_allreduce_smoke.py'
      EOF
      if [ -n "{{STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" ]; then
        cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/azcp-upload-user-data.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/bin/bash -l
      #SBATCH --nodes=1 --ntasks=1 --time=00:30:00
      #SBATCH --output=/shared/home/%u/azcp-upload-%j.out
      set -euo pipefail
      azcp copy "/shared/home/${USER}/datasets/" \
        "${AZCLUSTER_USER_BLOB_URL}/datasets/" --recursive \
        --workers 4 --concurrency 32 --block-size 16777216
      EOF
        sed -i 's|^      ||' "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/azcp-upload-user-data.sbatch"

        cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/azcp-build-and-publish-sqsh.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/bin/bash -l
      #SBATCH --nodes=1 --ntasks=1 --time=03:00:00
      #SBATCH --output=/shared/home/%u/build-sqsh-%j.out
      set -euo pipefail
      SQSH_NAME="${1:?usage: sbatch <script> <sqsh-name> <docker-url>}"
      DOCKER_URL="${2:?usage: sbatch <script> <sqsh-name> <docker-url>}"
      mkdir -p "${AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME}/sqsh"
      time enroot import -o "${AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME}/sqsh/${SQSH_NAME}.sqsh" "docker://${DOCKER_URL}"
      # Upload into a per-sqsh subdir so azcp-cluster (prefix-based) sees this
      # as a single-file directory and bcasts cleanly.
      azcp copy "${AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME}/sqsh/${SQSH_NAME}.sqsh" \
        "${AZCLUSTER_USER_BLOB_URL}/sqsh/${SQSH_NAME}/${SQSH_NAME}.sqsh" --no-progress
      EOF
        sed -i 's|^      ||' "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/azcp-build-and-publish-sqsh.sbatch"

        cat > "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/azcp-cluster-distribute-sqsh.sbatch" <<'EOF'
      #!/bin/bash -l
      #SBATCH --partition=gpu --nodes=2 --ntasks-per-node=1 --time=01:00:00 --exclusive
      #SBATCH --output=/shared/home/%u/distribute-%j.out
      set -euo pipefail
      SQSH_NAME="${1:?usage: sbatch <script> <sqsh-name>}"
      mkdir -p "${AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME}/sqsh"
      SRC="${AZCLUSTER_USER_BLOB_URL}/sqsh/${SQSH_NAME}/"
      DST="${AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME}/sqsh/${SQSH_NAME}/"
      mkdir -p "${DST}"
      # Tuning from https://github.com/edwardsp/azcp/blob/main/docs/cluster-h100-tuning.md
      # - UCX_TLS=rc,sm,self forces RDMA-over-IB (no TCP fallback)
      # - UCX_NET_DEVICES=mlx5_ib0:1 single-rail; multi-rail competes for the host bridge
      # - taskset -c 0-47 pins to NUMA-0; the frontend NIC and mlx5_ib0..3 are both on NUMA-0
      # - --bcast-pipeline 128 / --bcast-writers 8 = doc-recommended depth + parallel NVMe writers
      # The /dev/infiniband bind mount is omitted; MELLANOX_VISIBLE_DEVICES=all in
      # /etc/enroot/environ.d/50-nccl.env triggers the enroot 99-mellanox.sh hook to bind them in.
      export UCX_TLS=rc,sm,self
      export UCX_NET_DEVICES=mlx5_ib0:1
      export OMPI_MCA_pml=ucx
      export OMPI_MCA_osc=ucx
      srun --mpi=pmix \
        --container-image="docker://ghcr.io/edwardsp/azcp/azcp-cluster:{{AZCP_VERSION}}" \
        --container-mounts=${AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME}:${AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME} \
        --export=ALL,AZURE_CLIENT_ID,AZCLUSTER_USER_BLOB_URL,AZCLUSTER_USER_NVME,UCX_TLS,UCX_NET_DEVICES,OMPI_MCA_pml,OMPI_MCA_osc \
        taskset -c 0-47 \
        azcp-cluster \
          "${SRC}" "${DST}" \
          --concurrency 32 --block-size 16777216 \
          --bcast-chunk 67108864 --bcast-pipeline 128 --bcast-writers 8 \
          --compare size --no-progress
      EOF
        sed -i 's|^      ||' "${EXAMPLES_DIR}/azcp-cluster-distribute-sqsh.sbatch"
      fi
      chmod 0755 "${EXAMPLES_DIR}"
      chmod 0644 "${EXAMPLES_DIR}"/*.sbatch
      chown -R azureuser:azureuser "${EXAMPLES_DIR}"

      BASE="https://github.com/${AZCLUSTER_REPO}/releases/download/${AZCLUSTER_VERSION}"
      cd /tmp
      TARBALL="azcluster-server-${AZCLUSTER_VERSION}-x86_64-linux.tar.gz"
      curl -fsSL --retry 8 --retry-delay 5 -O "${BASE}/${TARBALL}"
      curl -fsSL --retry 8 --retry-delay 5 -O "${BASE}/SHA256SUMS"
      sha256sum --ignore-missing -c SHA256SUMS
      tar -xzf "${TARBALL}" -C /usr/local/bin/
      chmod +x /usr/local/bin/azcluster-server

      RG=$(curl -fsSL -H Metadata:true "http://169.254.169.254/metadata/instance/compute/resourceGroupName?api-version=2021-02-01&format=text")
      mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/azcluster-server.service.d
      cat > /etc/systemd/system/azcluster-server.service.d/resource-group.conf <<EOF
      [Service]
      Environment=AZCLUSTER_RESOURCE_GROUP=${RG}
      EOF

      # Azure CLI from MS repo (NOT via aka.ms/InstallAzureCLIDeb pipe — that
      # script invokes apt without lock-timeout and races apt-daily on busy boots,
      # killing the whole bootstrap under `set -euo pipefail`).
      AZ_CODENAME=$(. /etc/os-release; echo "${VERSION_CODENAME}")
      echo "deb [arch=$(dpkg --print-architecture) signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/microsoft-prod.gpg] https://packages.microsoft.com/repos/azure-cli/ ${AZ_CODENAME} main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/azure-cli.list
      apt-get -o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=600 update
      apt-get -o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=600 install -y --no-install-recommends azure-cli
      # Defensive login: scheduler UAI may have no subscription RBAC under
      # --no-monitoring --no-accounting test mode. No `az` calls follow on
      # the scheduler itself, so a failed login is tolerable.
      az login --identity --client-id {{UAI_CLIENT_ID}} --only-show-errors >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

      NODE_EXP_VER=1.8.2
      cd /tmp
      curl -fsSL --retry 8 --retry-delay 5 -O "https://github.com/prometheus/node_exporter/releases/download/v${NODE_EXP_VER}/node_exporter-${NODE_EXP_VER}.linux-amd64.tar.gz"
      tar -xzf "node_exporter-${NODE_EXP_VER}.linux-amd64.tar.gz"
      install -m 0755 "node_exporter-${NODE_EXP_VER}.linux-amd64/node_exporter" /usr/local/bin/node_exporter
      id -u node_exporter >/dev/null 2>&1 || useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin node_exporter
      cat > /etc/systemd/system/node_exporter.service <<'EOF'
      [Unit]
      Description=Prometheus Node Exporter
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target
      [Service]
      User=node_exporter
      ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/node_exporter --web.listen-address=127.0.0.1:9100
      Restart=on-failure
      RestartSec=5
      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target
      EOF

      systemctl daemon-reload
      systemctl enable munge
      systemctl restart munge
      systemctl enable --now node_exporter

      SLURM_EXP_VER="1.8.0"
      cd /tmp
      curl -fsSL --retry 8 --retry-delay 5 -o /tmp/prometheus-slurm-exporter.deb \
        "https://github.com/rivosinc/prometheus-slurm-exporter/releases/download/v${SLURM_EXP_VER}/prometheus-slurm-exporter_${SLURM_EXP_VER}_linux_amd64.deb"
      dpkg -i /tmp/prometheus-slurm-exporter.deb || true
      rm -f /tmp/prometheus-slurm-exporter.deb
      cat > /etc/systemd/system/prometheus-slurm-exporter.service <<'EOF'
      [Unit]
      Description=Prometheus SLURM Exporter
      After=network.target slurmctld.service
      Wants=slurmctld.service

      [Service]
      Type=simple
      User=slurm
      ExecStart=/usr/bin/prometheus-slurm-exporter
      Restart=always
      RestartSec=15
      Environment="POLL_LIMIT=30"
      Environment="LOGLEVEL=info"

      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target
      EOF
      chmod 0644 /etc/systemd/system/prometheus-slurm-exporter.service
      systemctl daemon-reload
      systemctl enable --now prometheus-slurm-exporter

      mkdir -p /opt/prometheus
      MON_UAI_CLIENT_ID="{{MON_UAI_CLIENT_ID}}"
      AMW_INGEST_URL="{{AMW_INGEST_URL}}"
      if [ -n "${MON_UAI_CLIENT_ID}" ] && [ -n "${AMW_INGEST_URL}" ]; then
        cat > /opt/prometheus/prometheus.yml <<EOF
      global:
        scrape_interval: 30s
        evaluation_interval: 30s
        external_labels:
          subscription: {{SUBSCRIPTION_ID}}
          cluster: ${RG}/{{CLUSTER_NAME}}
          nodename: $(hostname)
          role: scheduler
      remote_write:
        - url: "${AMW_INGEST_URL}"
          azuread:
            cloud: AzurePublic
            managed_identity:
              client_id: ${MON_UAI_CLIENT_ID}
      scrape_configs:
        - job_name: node_exporter
          static_configs:
            - targets: ['127.0.0.1:9100']
        - job_name: slurm_exporter
          scrape_interval: 60s
          scrape_timeout: 30s
          static_configs:
            - targets: ['127.0.0.1:9092']
      EOF
        chmod 0644 /opt/prometheus/prometheus.yml
        PROM_VER="2.55.1"
        curl -fsSL --retry 8 --retry-delay 5 -O \
          "https://github.com/prometheus/prometheus/releases/download/v${PROM_VER}/prometheus-${PROM_VER}.linux-amd64.tar.gz"
        tar -xzf "prometheus-${PROM_VER}.linux-amd64.tar.gz"
        install -m 0755 "prometheus-${PROM_VER}.linux-amd64/prometheus" /opt/prometheus/prometheus
        id -u prometheus >/dev/null 2>&1 || useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin prometheus
        chown -R prometheus:prometheus /opt/prometheus
        mkdir -p /var/lib/prometheus
        chown -R prometheus:prometheus /var/lib/prometheus
        cat > /etc/systemd/system/prometheus.service <<'EOF'
      [Unit]
      Description=Prometheus
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target

      [Service]
      Type=simple
      User=prometheus
      ExecStart=/opt/prometheus/prometheus \
        --config.file=/opt/prometheus/prometheus.yml \
        --storage.tsdb.path=/var/lib/prometheus \
        --web.listen-address=127.0.0.1:9090
      Restart=always
      RestartSec=15

      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target
      EOF
        chmod 0644 /etc/systemd/system/prometheus.service
        systemctl daemon-reload
        systemctl enable --now prometheus
      fi

      if [ "${ENABLE_ACCOUNTING:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        # Azure MySQL Flex serves a DigiCert Global Root CA chain, which is
        # already in Ubuntu's ca-certificates bundle. Point SSL_CA at the
        # system bundle rather than re-downloading (dl.cacerts.digicert.com
        # serves the wrong SNI cert and breaks curl).

        ACCT_PASSWORD=$(cat /etc/azcluster/accounting.password)
        cat > /etc/slurm/slurmdbd.conf <<EOF
      AuthType=auth/munge
      DbdHost=localhost
      DbdPort=6819
      SlurmUser=slurm
      DebugLevel=info
      LogFile=/var/log/slurm/slurmdbd.log
      PidFile=/var/run/slurmdbd.pid
      StorageType=accounting_storage/mysql
      StorageHost=${ACCT_MYSQL_FQDN}
      StoragePort=3306
      StorageUser=${ACCT_MYSQL_USER}
      StoragePass=${ACCT_PASSWORD}
      StorageLoc=${ACCT_MYSQL_DB}
      StorageParameters=SSL_CA=/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
      EOF
        chown slurm:slurm /etc/slurm/slurmdbd.conf
        chmod 0600 /etc/slurm/slurmdbd.conf
        unset ACCT_PASSWORD

        # Wait for MySQL Flex to be reachable on :3306 before starting slurmdbd.
        for i in $(seq 1 60); do
          if (echo > /dev/tcp/${ACCT_MYSQL_FQDN}/3306) >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            break
          fi
          sleep 5
        done

        systemctl enable --now slurmdbd
        # Give slurmdbd a moment to open its socket before slurmctld registers.
        sleep 5
      fi

      systemctl enable --now slurmctld
      systemctl enable --now azcluster-server.service

      if [ "${ENABLE_ACCOUNTING:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        # Register the cluster with slurmdbd (no-op if already auto-registered).
        for i in $(seq 1 12); do
          if sacctmgr -i show cluster "${AZCLUSTER_NAME}" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "${AZCLUSTER_NAME}"; then
            break
          fi
          sacctmgr -i add cluster "${AZCLUSTER_NAME}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
          sleep 5
        done
        # AccountingStorageEnforce=associations refuses job submission unless
        # every (user, account, cluster) tuple is registered. Seed a default
        # account + the standard azureuser association so submits work out of
        # the box. Future LDAP/Entra integration replaces the user step.
        sacctmgr -i add account default Description="Default account" Organization=azcluster >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
        sacctmgr -i add user azureuser DefaultAccount=default >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
      fi

      LDAP_ADMIN_PW=$(cat /etc/azcluster/ldap.password)
      export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
      printf '%s\n' \
        "slapd slapd/internal/adminpw password ${LDAP_ADMIN_PW}" \
        "slapd slapd/internal/generated_adminpw password ${LDAP_ADMIN_PW}" \
        "slapd slapd/password1 password ${LDAP_ADMIN_PW}" \
        "slapd slapd/password2 password ${LDAP_ADMIN_PW}" \
        "slapd slapd/domain string azcluster.local" \
        "slapd shared/organization string azcluster" \
        "slapd slapd/backend select MDB" \
        "slapd slapd/purge_database boolean true" \
        "slapd slapd/move_old_database boolean true" \
        "slapd slapd/no_configuration boolean false" \
        | debconf-set-selections
      apt-get -o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=600 install -y slapd
      for i in $(seq 1 30); do
        if ldapsearch -Y EXTERNAL -H ldapi:/// -b cn=schema,cn=config dn 2>/dev/null | grep -q '^dn: cn=.*core,cn=schema'; then
          break
        fi
        sleep 2
      done

      set +e
      ldapadd -Y EXTERNAL -H ldapi:/// -f /etc/azcluster/openssh-lpk.ldif
      rc=$?
      set -e
      if [ $rc -ne 0 ] && [ $rc -ne 68 ]; then
        echo "openssh-lpk schema add failed (rc=$rc)" >&2
        exit $rc
      fi

      BASE_LDIF=/etc/azcluster/ldap-base.ldif
      sed -e 's|__BASE_DN__|dc=azcluster,dc=local|g' \
          -e "s|__ADMIN_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY__|${ADMIN_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}|g" \
          /etc/azcluster/ldap-base.ldif.tmpl > ${BASE_LDIF}
      set +e
      ldapadd -x -D "cn=admin,dc=azcluster,dc=local" -w "${LDAP_ADMIN_PW}" -H ldap://127.0.0.1 -c -f ${BASE_LDIF}
      rc=$?
      set -e
      if [ $rc -ne 0 ] && [ $rc -ne 68 ]; then
        echo "base LDIF add failed (rc=$rc)" >&2
        exit $rc
      fi

      sed -i 's|^SLAPD_SERVICES=.*|SLAPD_SERVICES="ldap:/// ldapi:///"|' /etc/default/slapd
      systemctl restart slapd
      systemctl enable slapd
      unset LDAP_ADMIN_PW

      DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive apt-get -o DPkg::Lock::Timeout=600 install -y \
        sssd sssd-ldap sssd-tools libnss-sss
      cat > /etc/sssd/sssd.conf <<EOF
      [sssd]
      domains = azcluster
      config_file_version = 2
      services = nss, pam

      [domain/azcluster]
      id_provider = ldap
      auth_provider = ldap
      ldap_uri = ldap://127.0.0.1
      ldap_search_base = dc=azcluster,dc=local
      enumerate = false
      cache_credentials = true
      override_homedir = /shared/home/%u
      entry_cache_timeout = 60
      entry_cache_user_timeout = 60
      EOF
      chmod 0600 /etc/sssd/sssd.conf
      chown root:root /etc/sssd/sssd.conf
      systemctl enable --now sssd
      systemctl restart slurmctld

      if [ "${ENABLE_ACCOUNTING:-false}" = "true" ]; then
        # v0.24.1: register clusteradmin + clusteruser with slurmdbd so
        # AccountingStorageEnforce=associations,limits,qos accepts their job
        # submissions. The base LDIF created these LDAP users; this loop adds
        # the matching sacctmgr accounts + per-partition associations.
        sleep 5
        for u in clusteradmin clusteruser; do
          sacctmgr -i add account "$u" Description="azcluster default user $u" Organization=azcluster >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
          sacctmgr -i add user "$u" DefaultAccount="$u" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
          for p in $(sinfo -h -o '%R' 2>/dev/null | sort -u); do
            sacctmgr -i add user "$u" Account="$u" Partition="$p" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
          done
        done
        systemctl restart slurmctld
      fi

      chmod 1777 /shared
      install -d -m 0755 -o root -g root /shared/home
      install -d -m 1777 -o root -g root /shared/dgxc
      install -d -m 1777 -o root -g root /shared/jobs

      touch /var/log/azcluster/ready

runcmd:
  - systemctl stop unattended-upgrades.service apt-daily.service apt-daily-upgrade.service 2>/dev/null || true
  - systemctl mask unattended-upgrades.service apt-daily.service apt-daily-upgrade.service apt-daily.timer apt-daily-upgrade.timer 2>/dev/null || true
  - bash -lc 'i=0; while fuser /var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend /var/lib/dpkg/lock /var/lib/apt/lists/lock >/dev/null 2>&1; do sleep 5; i=$((i+1)); [ $i -gt 120 ] && break; done'
  - bash -lc 'mkdir -p /var/log/azcluster && /opt/azcluster/install-scheduler.sh 2>&1 | tee -a /var/log/azcluster/install.log'
variables.cloudInit: [replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(variables('cloudInitTemplate'), '{{AZCLUSTER_VERSION}}', parameters('azclusterVersion')), '{{AZCLUSTER_REPO}}', parameters('azclusterRepo')), '{{ADMIN_USER}}', parameters('adminUsername')), '{{CLUSTER_NAME}}', parameters('clusterName')), '{{SHARED_MOUNT_IP}}', parameters('sharedMountIp')), '{{SHARED_EXPORT_PATH}}', parameters('sharedExportPath')), '{{RUN_NFS_SERVER}}', if(parameters('runNfsServer'), 'true', 'false')), '{{PARTITIONS}}', parameters('partitionsConf')), '{{UAI_CLIENT_ID}}', parameters('userAssignedIdentityClientId')), '{{MON_UAI_CLIENT_ID}}', parameters('monUaiClientId')), '{{AMW_INGEST_URL}}', parameters('amwIngestionEndpoint')), '{{SUBSCRIPTION_ID}}', subscription().subscriptionId), '{{ENABLE_ACCOUNTING}}', if(parameters('enableAccounting'), 'true', 'false')), '{{ACCT_MYSQL_FQDN}}', parameters('accountingMysqlFqdn')), '{{ACCT_MYSQL_USER}}', parameters('accountingMysqlUser')), '{{ACCT_MYSQL_DB}}', parameters('accountingMysqlDatabase')), '{{ACCT_MYSQL_PASSWORD}}', parameters('mysqlAdminPassword')), '{{LDAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD}}', parameters('ldapAdminPassword')), '{{EXTRA_PACKAGES}}', parameters('extraPackages')), '{{STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}', parameters('storageAccountName')), '{{STORAGE_BLOB_ENDPOINT}}', parameters('storageBlobEndpoint')), '{{STORAGE_DATA_CONTAINER_URL}}', parameters('storageDataContainerUrl')), '{{AZCP_VERSION}}', parameters('azcpVersion')), '{{ADMIN_SSH_PUBLIC_KEY}}', parameters('sshPublicKey'))]
